step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the conclusion that the highest risk associated with the use of oral contraceptives is thromboembolic disorders. One additional point worth noting is that while liver function tests may occasionally reveal mild abnormalities, hepatic necrosis is extremely rare. However, given the serious nature of thromboembolic events—potentially life-threatening complications like Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism (PE)—it justifies why they are emphasized more strongly during discussions around safety profiles of oral contraceptives.
raw_response:
I agree with the conclusion that the highest risk associated with the use of oral contraceptives is thromboembolic disorders. One additional point worth noting is that while liver function tests may occasionally reveal mild abnormalities, hepatic necrosis is extremely rare. However, given the serious nature of thromboembolic events—potentially life-threatening complications like Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism (PE)—it justifies why they are emphasized more strongly during discussions around safety profiles of oral contraceptives.

Answer: C.
